data.title: Bitcoin's rally halts at $90K; persistent outflows from ETFs suggest potential breakthrough at $93,000 resistance ahead.
data.description: Significant outflows from Bitcoin Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) have decreased, indicating a positive trend for BTC price to potentially surpass the upcoming obstacle at $93,000.
Putin announced plans to open a Russian university in the UAE under the auspices of the "Sirius" center.

data.title: PulteGroup's Q4 2025 net income $502 million, $2.56 per share, with $81M charge for divestiture and $35M land impairment.
data.description: In Atlanta, PulteGroup, Inc. released its financial results for the fourth quarter ending on December 31, 2025. The company reported a net income of $502 million, which translates to $2.56 per share. Within this figure, a pre-tax charge of $81 million (equivalent to $0.31 per share) was recorded for the divestiture of specific manufacturing assets. Additionally, there were pre-tax land impairment charges amounting to $35 million ($0.14 per share), along with a pre-tax insurance benefit.

data.title: Eagle Materials Q3 2025: Revenue $556m, Net Earnings $102.9m, EPS $3.22, Adjusted EBITDA $190.1m.
data.description: Eagle Materials Inc. (NYSE: EXP) announced its financial results for the third quarter of fiscal 2026 ending on December 31, 2025. In this quarter, the company reported: Revenue of $556.0 million, Net Earnings of $102.9 million, Net Earnings per share of $3.22, and Adjusted EBITDA of $190.1 million. These figures represent a comparison with the prior year’s fiscal third quarter. Adjusted EBITDA is considered a non-GAAP financial measure.

data.title: Trane Technologies (NYSE:TT) reports Q4 2025 diluted EPS of $2.74, adjusted EPS $2.86 (10% increase). Q4 bookings $5.76B, 24% Y-O-Y change. Net revenues $5.145B.
data.description: Trane Technologies plc, a leading global climate innovator, announced diluted earnings per share (EPS) from continuing operations of $2.74 for the fourth quarter of 2025. The adjusted continuing EPS stood at $2.86, marking a 10% increase. In a year-over-year (YOY) comparison, fourth-quarter results for 2025 showed significant growth: bookings increased by 24% to $5,760 million from $4,659 million in 2024, representing a 22% organic YOY change. Net revenues also saw a rise, reaching $5,145 million compared to $4,874 million in 2024, showcasing a 6% overall increase and a 4% organic YOY change.

data.title: Carvel® Ice Cream Cakes introduces new look to attract next-gen cake lovers. Revamped packaging, logo, and tagline aim to enhance brand appeal.
data.description: Carvel®, an iconic ice cream cake brand, has recently unveiled a refreshed look with updated packaging, logo, and tagline, aiming to appeal to a new generation of ice cream cake enthusiasts. The unveiling took place in Buffalo, N.Y. on January 29, 2026. The company's whimsical energy is now prominently showcased, promising a more irresistible experience for customers. This update signifies a new chapter for Carvel®, ensuring its continued popularity among consumers.

data.title: EIFS market to reach $67.38 billion by 2030 per MarketsandMarkets™ report, featuring PB and PM types with EPS, MW insulation.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, in Delray Beach, Florida, it was reported by a market research firm that the Exterior Insulation and Finish System (EIFS) Market is expected to witness significant growth. The market is segmented by Type into Polymer-based (PB) and Polymer-modified (PM), and by Insulation Type into Expanded Polystyrene (EPS), Mineral Wool (MW), and Other Insulation Types. According to the report by MarketsandMarkets, the EIFS market is projected to experience substantial expansion, with key numbers and figures indicating a promising outlook for the industry.

data.title: CSW Industrials (NYSE: CSW) reports record results for fiscal 2026 third quarter ended on December 31, 2025.
data.description: On January 29, 2026, CSW Industrials, Inc. disclosed its financial outcomes for the fiscal third quarter of 2026, which concluded on December 31, 2025. The company, identified on the NYSE as CSW, provided this update.

data.title: Xerox reports Q4 and full-year results, anticipating growth in 2026. Positive outlook despite challenges.
data.description: Xerox Holdings Corporation, listed on the NASDAQ as XRX, released its 2025 fourth-quarter and full-year financial results alongside projections for 2026. Despite challenges like government uncertainty and increased memory costs, CEO Steve Bandrowczak highlighted their disciplined execution. The integration of Lexmark is progressing faster than expected, with teams achieving significant synergies. Xerox remains resilient in navigating the demanding economic landscape.

data.title: Valero Energy Corp. reports $1.1 billion net income in Q4 2025 vs $281 million in Q4 2024, $3.73 per share vs $0.88.
data.description: In San Antonio, Valero Energy Corporation (NYSE: VLO, “Valero”) announced a significant increase in net income attributable to Valero stockholders for the fourth quarter of 2025. The company reported a net income of $1.1 billion, or $3.73 per share, compared to $281 million, or $0.88 per share, in the same quarter of 2024. Adjusted for certain items, the adjusted net income attributable to Valero stockholders was $1.2 billion, or $3.82 per share, for the fourth quarter of 2025. This represents a notable growth in earnings from the previous year.

data.title: Ares provides $1.6B in debt financing for Suave Brands and Elida Beauty merger, creating Evermark.
data.description: Ares Management Corporation (NYSE: ARES) disclosed today that its Ares Credit funds acted as the administrative agent for $1.6 billion in debt financing to facilitate the merger of Suave Brands Company and Elida Beauty, both portfolio companies of Yellow Wood Partners. The merger resulted in the formation of Evermark, LLC, a new entity in the industry. Evermark emerges as a prominent global platform following the integration, aiming to become a frontrunner in its sector.
